title: File-Based Routing in Nuxt
content: ### pages/ Directory In Nuxt, every `.vue` file in the `pages/` directory automatically becomes a route — no router configuration needed. ### index.vue ``` pages/ index.vue → / about.vue → /about contact.vue → /contact ``` ### Nested Directories Create nested routes by nesting directories: ``` pages/ blog/ index.vue → /blog [id].vue → /blog/:id dashboard/ settings.vue → /dashboard/settings profile.vue → /dashboard/profile ``` ### Dynamic Routes Use square brackets for dynamic segments: ``` pages/ users/ [id].vue → /users/:id (params.id) [id]/edit.vue → /users/:id/edit [group]/[role].vue → /users/:group/:role ``` Access params: ```vue ``` ### Navigation Use `` instead of ``: ```vue About User External Blog ``` ### Route Middleware Create middleware in `middleware/`: ```js // middleware/auth.ts export default defineNuxtRouteMiddleware((to, from) => { const token = useCookie('token') if (!token.value) { return navigateTo('/login') } }) ``` Apply it: ```vue ```
codeExample: pages/ ├── index.vue # / ├── about.vue # /about ├── blog/ │ ├── index.vue # /blog │ └── [slug].vue # /blog/:slug ├── products/ │ ├── index.vue # /products │ └── [id].vue # /products/:id └── dashboard/ ├── index.vue # /dashboard ├── settings.vue # /dashboard/settings └── [user].vue # /dashboard/:user

quiz.question: How are routes defined in Nuxt 3?
What syntax creates a dynamic route in Nuxt?
